name: Activity Feed & Cost Tracking Pages
description: Activity feed full page: chronological event list auto-updating via SSE (useEventSource hook), filterable by agent/project/event type (AC7). Cost & Token dashboard page: per-project and per-agent cost breakdown bar charts, token usage over time line charts (recharts), budget alert configuration stored in SQLite. Gracefully shows 'no data available' placeholder UI when token/cost data is missing from filesystem (AC8). Overview/home dashboard page that aggregates: agent count + status summary cards, active projects count, PACT health snapshot, last 10 activity events, cost burn rate. Uses Zod-first approach where Zod schemas are single source of truth with TypeScript types inferred via z.infer. All timestamps ISO 8601 strings. All monetary values as integer microdollars (1 USD = 1,000,000 microdollars) formatted to dollars only at UI presentation layer.

types.name: ActivityEventType
types.description: Dot-notation taxonomy of all activity event types, used as discriminant for ActivityEvent union.

types.name: DataResultStatus
types.description: Status discriminant for DataResult<T> wrapper. 'ok' = data present, 'empty' = no data found (graceful empty state for AC8), 'error' = retrieval failed.

types.name: DataResultOk

fields.name: status
fields.description: Always 'ok'.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The successfully retrieved data payload. Generic T in implementation; typed per usage site via Zod schema inference.

types.description: DataResult variant when data is successfully retrieved.

types.name: DataResultEmpty

fields.name: status
fields.description: Always 'empty'.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Human-readable reason why data is empty, e.g. 'No cost data files found for project X'.

types.description: DataResult variant when no data is available (e.g., filesystem cost files missing). Used to trigger 'no data available' placeholder UI (AC8).

types.name: DataResultError

fields.name: status
fields.description: Always 'error'.

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code, e.g. 'FILE_READ_FAILED', 'PARSE_ERROR', 'DB_QUERY_FAILED'.

fields.name: error_message
fields.description: Human-readable error description.

types.description: DataResult variant when data retrieval failed due to an error.

types.name: DataResult
types.description: Generic wrapper with 'ok' | 'empty' | 'error' status for graceful empty states. All data layer functions return this. Discriminated on 'status' field.

types.name: ActivityEvent

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique event ID (UUID v4). Used for SSE deduplication via lastEventId.

validators.error_message: Event ID must be a valid UUID v4

fields.name: type
fields.description: Dot-notation event type, used as discriminant for event-specific payloads.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event occurred.

fields.name: agent_id
fields.description: ID of the agent that generated this event, if applicable.

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: ID of the project this event relates to, if applicable.

fields.name: task_id
fields.description: ID of the task this event relates to, if applicable.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Human-readable one-line summary of the event.

fields.name: details
fields.description: Event-type-specific structured details. Schema varies by event type; validated per-type via Zod discriminated union.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Event severity level for visual treatment in the feed.

types.description: A single activity event in the chronological feed. Parsed from filesystem event logs via Zod schema. IDs are UUIDs for SSE deduplication.

types.name: EventSeverity
types.description: Severity level for activity events, controls visual treatment in the UI feed.

types.name: ActivityFilter

fields.name: agent_ids
fields.description: Filter to events from these agent IDs. Empty or omitted = all agents.

fields.name: project_ids
fields.description: Filter to events for these project IDs. Empty or omitted = all projects.

fields.name: event_types
fields.description: Filter to these event types. Empty or omitted = all types.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Filter by severity levels. Empty or omitted = all severities.

fields.name: since
fields.description: Only events after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

fields.name: until
fields.description: Only events before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

types.description: First-class filter object for the activity feed. Used both in API query params (validated via Zod) and in SSE subscription filters.

types.name: StringList
types.description: A list of strings.

types.name: ActivityEventTypeList
types.description: A list of ActivityEventType enum values for filtering.

types.name: EventSeverityList
types.description: A list of EventSeverity enum values for filtering.

types.name: CursorPagination

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Event ID cursor for pagination. Empty string or omitted = start from most recent.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Maximum number of items to return per page.

types.description: Cursor-based pagination parameters. Cursor is the event ID of the last item in the previous page.

types.name: ActivityEventList
types.description: Ordered list of activity events (most recent first).

types.name: ActivityFeedResponse

fields.name: events
fields.description: Activity events for the current page, ordered most recent first.

fields.name: next_cursor
fields.description: Cursor for the next page. Empty string if no more pages.

fields.name: has_more
fields.description: Whether there are more events beyond this page.

fields.name: total_count
fields.description: Total count of events matching the filter, if available. May be omitted for performance on large datasets.

types.description: Cursor-paginated activity feed API response.

types.name: CostRecord

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique cost record ID.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the invocation.

fields.name: agent_id
fields.description: Agent that incurred this cost.

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: Project the cost is attributed to.

fields.name: model
fields.description: LLM model identifier, e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514'.

fields.name: input_tokens
fields.description: Number of input/prompt tokens.

fields.name: output_tokens
fields.description: Number of output/completion tokens.

fields.name: cost_microdollars
fields.description: Cost in microdollars (1 USD = 1,000,000). Integer to avoid floating-point precision issues.

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: OpenClaw session ID, if available.

fields.name: task_id
fields.description: Task ID this invocation was part of, if available.

types.description: A single per-invocation cost record. Read from filesystem cost logs. All monetary values in integer microdollars (1 USD = 1,000,000 microdollars).

types.name: TokenUsage

fields.name: period_start
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for start of this aggregation period.

fields.name: period_end
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for end of this aggregation period.

fields.name: input_tokens
fields.description: Total input tokens in this period.

fields.name: output_tokens
fields.description: Total output tokens in this period.

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: Sum of input + output tokens.

fields.name: invocation_count
fields.description: Number of LLM invocations in this period.

types.name: BudgetAlert

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique alert ID (UUID v4).

validators.error_message: Alert ID must be a valid UUID v4

fields.name: name
fields.description: User-defined alert name.

validators.error_message: Alert name must be between 1 and 200 characters

fields.name: scope_type
fields.description: Whether this alert applies to a specific project, agent, or globally.

fields.name: scope_id
fields.description: The project_id or agent_id this alert scopes to. Empty for global scope.

fields.name: threshold_microdollars
fields.description: Spending threshold in microdollars. Alert fires when cumulative cost crosses this value.

validators.error_message: Threshold must be a positive value in microdollars

fields.name: period
fields.description: Time period over which spending is aggregated for threshold comparison.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether this alert is currently active.

fields.name: last_triggered_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this alert last fired. Used for debounce to avoid repeated notifications.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of alert creation.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last alert modification.

types.name: BudgetAlertScopeType

fields.name: name

validators.error_message: Alert name must be between 1 and 200 characters

fields.name: scope_type

fields.name: scope_id

fields.name: threshold_microdollars

validators.error_message: Threshold must be a positive value in microdollars

fields.name: period
fields.description: Aggregation period for threshold comparison.

fields.name: enabled

fields.name: name

types.description: Input for updating an existing budget alert. All fields optional (partial update).

types.description: List of budget alert configurations.

fields.name: deleted_id

types.name: SSEFilterParams

fields.name: agent_ids
fields.description: Comma-separated agent IDs to filter on.

fields.name: project_ids

fields.name: event_types
fields.description: Comma-separated event types to filter on.

fields.name: last_event_id
fields.description: Last received event ID for reconnection. Server replays missed events since this ID.

types.description: Query parameters for subscribing to the multiplexed SSE endpoint at /api/events. All filters are optional; omitted = no filter on that dimension.

types.name: SSEMessage

fields.name: id
fields.description: Event ID for lastEventId tracking. Same as ActivityEvent.id for activity events.

fields.name: event
fields.description: SSE event type field. Uses the ActivityEventType dot-notation or 'heartbeat' for keepalive.

fields.name: data
fields.description: JSON-serialized event payload. Parsed by client via Zod schema.

fields.name: retry
fields.description: Suggested reconnection delay in milliseconds. Only sent on initial connection.

types.description: A single SSE message sent to the client. Conforms to the SSE protocol with id, event, and data fields.

types.name: CostRecordList
types.description: List of cost records.

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: Filter by project ID.

fields.name: agent_id

fields.name: period_start
fields.description: ISO 8601 start of query period.

fields.name: period_end
fields.description: ISO 8601 end of query period.

fields.name: granularity
fields.description: Time bucket granularity for time series data.

types.description: Query parameters for cost data retrieval.

functions.name: listActivityEvents
functions.description: Retrieves a cursor-paginated list of activity events from the filesystem event logs, filtered by the provided criteria. Events are read from OpenClaw/PACT event log files via the data access layer. Returns DataResult wrapping ActivityFeedResponse.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Filter criteria for events. Omit for unfiltered.

inputs.name: pagination
inputs.description: Cursor-based pagination parameters. Defaults to first 50 events.

error_cases.name: INVALID_CURSOR
error_cases.condition: Provided cursor ID does not exist in event logs

error_cases.name: EVENT_LOG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Filesystem event log files exist but could not be read

error_cases.name: EVENT_LOG_PARSE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Event log file contents failed Zod schema validation

error_cases.name: INVALID_FILTER
error_cases.condition: Filter parameters fail Zod validation

preconditions: If pagination.cursor is provided, it must be a valid event ID that exists in the event log
If filter.since is provided, it must be a valid ISO 8601 timestamp
If filter.until is provided, it must be a valid ISO 8601 timestamp and >= filter.since
postconditions: On status='ok', data contains ActivityFeedResponse with events.length <= pagination.limit
Events are ordered by timestamp descending (most recent first)
On status='ok', if has_more is true then next_cursor is a non-empty string
On status='empty', no event log files were found on the filesystem
All returned events match the provided filter criteria

structured_side_effects.description: Reads event log files from filesystem via data access layer

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total events scanned for filtering, O(1) with cursor index

functions.name: getActivityEventById
functions.description: Retrieves a single activity event by its unique ID.

inputs.name: event_id
inputs.description: UUID v4 event ID to retrieve.

validators.error_message: Event ID must be a valid UUID v4

error_cases.name: INVALID_EVENT_ID
error_cases.condition: event_id is not a valid UUID v4

error_cases.name: EVENT_LOG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Filesystem event log files could not be read

preconditions: event_id is a valid UUID v4 string
postconditions: On status='ok', data contains a single ActivityEvent with matching id
On status='empty', no event with that ID exists

structured_side_effects.description: Reads event log files to find the specific event

functions.name: aggregateCostData
functions.description: Aggregates cost data from filesystem cost log files into the CostDashboardResponse structure. Produces per-project breakdowns, per-agent breakdowns, and time-bucketed series. Returns DataResult wrapping CostDashboardResponse. Gracefully returns 'empty' status when no cost files are found (AC8).

inputs.name: params
inputs.description: Query parameters for filtering and time bucketing. Omit for all data with daily granularity.

error_cases.name: COST_LOG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Cost log files exist but could not be read from filesystem

error_cases.name: COST_LOG_PARSE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Cost log file contents failed Zod schema validation

error_cases.name: INVALID_QUERY_PARAMS
error_cases.condition: Query parameters fail Zod validation

preconditions: If params.period_start is provided, it must be a valid ISO 8601 timestamp
If params.period_end is provided, it must be >= params.period_start
postconditions: On status='ok', data contains CostDashboardResponse with all sections populated
On status='ok', total_cost_microdollars equals sum of all by_project entries
On status='ok', time_series data points are ordered chronologically
On status='empty', no cost log files were found on the filesystem — UI should show 'no data available' placeholder (AC8)
Each CostBreakdownEntry.total_cost_microdollars is the sum of its constituent CostRecords
Monetary values are always integer microdollars, never floating-point

structured_side_effects.description: Reads cost/token log files from filesystem via data access layer

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total cost records in the queried period

functions.name: listCostRecords
functions.description: Lists raw cost records from filesystem, filtered by project/agent/time. Lower-level than aggregateCostData — used for detailed drill-down views.

inputs.name: params
inputs.description: Query parameters for filtering.

inputs.name: pagination
inputs.description: Cursor-based pagination. Defaults to first 50 records.

error_cases.name: COST_LOG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Cost log files could not be read

error_cases.name: COST_LOG_PARSE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Cost log contents malformed

postconditions: On status='ok', data contains list of CostRecord ordered by timestamp descending
On status='empty', no cost records match the filter criteria or no cost files found
All returned records match the provided filter criteria

structured_side_effects.description: Reads cost log files from filesystem

functions.name: aggregateTokenUsage
functions.description: Aggregates token usage data into time-bucketed series for charting. Returns DataResult wrapping TokenUsageList.

inputs.name: params
inputs.description: Query parameters including granularity and time range.

error_cases.name: COST_LOG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Cost log files could not be read

error_cases.name: COST_LOG_PARSE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Cost log contents malformed

preconditions: params.granularity is a valid TimeBucketGranularity value
postconditions: On status='ok', data contains TokenUsageList ordered chronologically
Each TokenUsage.total_tokens equals input_tokens + output_tokens
On status='empty', no token data found for the queried period

structured_side_effects.description: Reads cost/token log files from filesystem

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total cost records in the queried period

functions.name: getBurnRate
functions.description: Calculates the current cost burn rate over a configurable time window. Used in the overview dashboard for the burn rate card.

inputs.name: window_hours
inputs.description: Time window in hours over which to calculate burn rate.

validators.error_message: Window must be between 1 and 720 hours

error_cases.name: COST_LOG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Cost log files could not be read

error_cases.name: INSUFFICIENT_DATA
error_cases.condition: Some cost data exists but not enough for trend calculation

preconditions: window_hours is between 1 and 720
postconditions: On status='ok', data contains BurnRate with rate calculated over the specified window
On status='ok', trend is calculated by comparing current window to the immediately preceding window of equal length
On status='empty', no cost data exists for the specified window

structured_side_effects.description: Reads recent cost data for burn rate calculation

functions.name: createBudgetAlert
functions.description: Creates a new budget alert configuration in SQLite. Validates input via Zod schema. Returns the created alert.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Alert creation parameters.

error_cases.name: VALIDATION_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Input fails Zod schema validation

error_cases.name: SCOPE_ID_REQUIRED
error_cases.condition: scope_type is 'project' or 'agent' but scope_id is empty

error_cases.name: SCOPE_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND
error_cases.condition: scope_id references a project or agent that doesn't exist

error_cases.name: DB_WRITE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: SQLite insert failed

preconditions: input passes Zod schema validation
If scope_type is 'project' or 'agent', scope_id must be non-empty
If scope_type is 'global', scope_id must be empty
postconditions: On success=true, alert is persisted in SQLite with a generated UUID v4 id
On success=true, alert.created_at and alert.updated_at are set to current ISO 8601 timestamp
On success=true, alert.last_triggered_at is empty string (never triggered)
On success=true, response.alert contains the complete persisted alert

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts a new budget alert row

functions.name: updateBudgetAlert
functions.description: Updates an existing budget alert configuration in SQLite. Partial update — only provided fields are modified.

inputs.name: alert_id
inputs.description: UUID of the alert to update.

validators.error_message: Alert ID must be a valid UUID v4

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Fields to update. Only non-null fields are applied.

error_cases.name: ALERT_NOT_FOUND
error_cases.condition: No alert exists with the given alert_id

error_cases.name: VALIDATION_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Update input fails Zod schema validation

error_cases.name: EMPTY_UPDATE
error_cases.condition: No fields provided in the update input

error_data.message: At least one field must be provided for update

error_cases.name: DB_WRITE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: SQLite update failed

preconditions: alert_id is a valid UUID v4
At least one field in input is provided (non-empty update)
postconditions: On success=true, alert.updated_at is set to current ISO 8601 timestamp
On success=true, only the provided fields are modified; other fields remain unchanged
On success=true, response.alert contains the complete updated alert
If enabled is changed from false to true, last_triggered_at is reset to empty

structured_side_effects.description: Updates an existing budget alert row

functions.name: deleteBudgetAlert
functions.description: Deletes a budget alert configuration from SQLite by ID.

inputs.name: alert_id
inputs.description: UUID of the alert to delete.

validators.error_message: Alert ID must be a valid UUID v4

error_cases.name: ALERT_NOT_FOUND
error_cases.condition: No alert exists with the given alert_id

error_cases.name: DB_WRITE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: SQLite delete failed

preconditions: alert_id is a valid UUID v4
postconditions: On success=true, the alert is removed from SQLite
On success=true, response.deleted_id equals the input alert_id
Subsequent getBudgetAlert(alert_id) returns not found

structured_side_effects.description: Deletes a budget alert row

functions.name: getBudgetAlert
functions.description: Retrieves a single budget alert configuration by ID from SQLite.

inputs.name: alert_id
inputs.description: UUID of the alert to retrieve.

validators.error_message: Alert ID must be a valid UUID v4

error_cases.name: ALERT_NOT_FOUND
error_cases.condition: No alert exists with the given alert_id

error_cases.name: DB_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: SQLite query failed

preconditions: alert_id is a valid UUID v4
postconditions: On success=true, response.alert contains the full BudgetAlert with matching ID
On success=false with ALERT_NOT_FOUND, no alert exists with that ID

structured_side_effects.description: Reads a budget alert row from SQLite database file

functions.name: listBudgetAlerts
functions.description: Lists all budget alert configurations from SQLite. Optionally filtered by scope.

inputs.name: scope_type
inputs.description: Filter alerts by scope type. Omit for all alerts.

inputs.name: scope_id
inputs.description: Filter alerts by scope entity ID. Requires scope_type.

inputs.name: enabled_only
inputs.description: If true, only return enabled alerts.

error_cases.name: INVALID_FILTER
error_cases.condition: scope_id provided without scope_type

error_data.message: scope_type required when scope_id is provided

error_cases.name: DB_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: SQLite query failed

preconditions: If scope_id is provided, scope_type must also be provided
postconditions: On success=true, response.alerts contains all matching BudgetAlerts ordered by created_at descending
If no alerts match, response.alerts is an empty list (still success=true)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads budget alert rows from SQLite database file

functions.name: evaluateBudgetAlerts
functions.description: Evaluates all enabled budget alerts against current cost data. For each alert whose threshold is crossed, updates last_triggered_at and emits a 'cost.threshold_crossed' SSE event. Called periodically by the data layer (e.g., on new cost data detection).

error_cases.name: DB_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Failed to read alerts from SQLite

error_cases.name: DB_WRITE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Failed to update last_triggered_at in SQLite

error_cases.name: COST_DATA_UNAVAILABLE
error_cases.condition: Cost data files could not be read for threshold evaluation

error_cases.name: SSE_EMIT_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Failed to emit SSE event for triggered alert

preconditions: SQLite database is accessible
Cost data files are accessible on filesystem
postconditions: Returns list of BudgetAlerts that were triggered in this evaluation
Each triggered alert has last_triggered_at updated to current ISO 8601 timestamp in SQLite
A 'cost.threshold_crossed' SSE event is emitted for each newly triggered alert
Alerts that were already triggered within their debounce window are not re-triggered
Alerts with enabled=false are skipped

structured_side_effects.description: Reads cost data for threshold evaluation
Reads enabled budget alerts
Updates last_triggered_at on triggered alerts
Emits cost.threshold_crossed events via SSE

performance_budget.complexity: O(a * n) where a is number of enabled alerts and n is cost records in the alert's period

functions.name: getDashboardOverview
functions.description: Assembles the composite dashboard overview by aggregating data from multiple sources: agent status from OpenClaw filesystem, active project count, PACT health from PACT CLI, last 10 activity events, and cost burn rate. Each section is independently fetched and wrapped in DataResult so partial failures don't block the entire dashboard.

error_cases.name: TOTAL_FAILURE
error_cases.condition: All data sources are simultaneously unavailable

error_data.message: All dashboard data sources are unavailable

postconditions: generated_at is set to current ISO 8601 timestamp
Each section (agent_summary, active_projects_count, pact_health, recent_activity, burn_rate) is independently a DataResult
If a section's data source is unavailable, that section returns DataResultEmpty or DataResultError while other sections still return normally
recent_activity contains at most 10 events on status='ok'
All monetary values in burn_rate are integer microdollars

structured_side_effects.description: Reads agent status data
Reads project data for active count
Reads PACT health/approval data
Reads recent activity events
Reads cost data for burn rate

functions.name: getAgentStatusSummary
functions.description: Reads agent configuration and session data from OpenClaw filesystem and produces an AgentStatusSummary. Used by getDashboardOverview and potentially standalone.

error_cases.name: AGENT_CONFIG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Agent configuration directory could not be read

error_cases.name: AGENT_SESSION_PARSE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Agent session files are malformed

postconditions: On status='ok', data contains AgentStatusSummary where total = active + idle + errored + stopped
On status='empty', no agent configuration files found

structured_side_effects.description: Reads agent config and session state files

functions.name: getPactHealthSnapshot
functions.description: Reads PACT state data from the filesystem and optionally calls the PACT CLI to produce a PactHealthSnapshot.

error_cases.name: PACT_STATE_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: PACT state directory could not be read

error_cases.name: PACT_CLI_FAILED
error_cases.condition: PACT CLI invocation failed or timed out

error_cases.name: PACT_STATE_PARSE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: PACT state files are malformed

postconditions: On status='ok', data contains PactHealthSnapshot with last_check_at set to current timestamp
On status='empty', PACT state directory does not exist or is empty
Approval/rejection/timeout counts cover the last 24 hours

structured_side_effects.description: Reads PACT state files from filesystem
Optionally invokes PACT CLI for health check

functions.name: subscribeToEvents
functions.description: Establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection at /api/events. Multiplexes all event types through a single endpoint. Supports filter query params and reconnection via lastEventId. The SSE stream sends heartbeat events every 30 seconds to keep the connection alive.

inputs.name: filters
inputs.description: Optional filter parameters from query string.

error_cases.name: INVALID_FILTER_PARAMS
error_cases.condition: Filter query params fail Zod validation

error_cases.name: EVENT_REPLAY_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Could not replay events from last_event_id (too old or invalid)

error_cases.name: STREAM_ERROR
error_cases.condition: SSE event bus encounters an internal error

preconditions: If last_event_id is provided, it should be a valid event ID for replay
Client supports SSE (EventSource API or compatible)
postconditions: Returns a streaming SSE response with Content-Type: text/event-stream
Each SSE message includes id, event, and data fields
Heartbeat events are sent every 30 seconds with event='heartbeat'
If last_event_id is provided, missed events since that ID are replayed before live streaming
Only events matching the provided filters are sent to this client
Initial message includes retry field suggesting 3000ms reconnection delay

structured_side_effects.description: Reads event logs for replay on reconnection
Subscribes to the internal SSE event bus for live events
Logs SSE connection/disconnection for monitoring

performance_budget.complexity: O(m) for initial replay where m is missed events; O(1) per live event

functions.name: transformCostRecordsToChartData
functions.description: Pure transformation function in lib/data/transforms.ts. Converts a list of CostRecords into CostChartDataPointList shaped for recharts consumption. Groups by the specified dimension (project, agent, or time bucket).

inputs.name: records
inputs.description: Raw cost records to transform.

inputs.name: group_by
inputs.description: Grouping dimension: 'project', 'agent', or 'time'.

inputs.name: granularity
inputs.description: Time bucket granularity. Only used when group_by='time'.

preconditions: records is a valid CostRecordList (may be empty)
group_by is one of 'project', 'agent', 'time'
postconditions: Output length equals number of unique groups in the input
Each data point's cost_dollars equals cost_microdollars / 1_000_000
If records is empty, returns empty list
When group_by='time', data points are ordered chronologically
When group_by='project' or 'agent', data points are ordered by cost descending

structured_side_effects.description: Pure transformation function with no side effects

functions.name: transformTokenUsageToChartData
functions.description: Pure transformation function in lib/data/transforms.ts. Converts TokenUsageList into TokenChartDataPointList shaped for recharts line chart consumption.

inputs.name: usage_data
inputs.description: Time-bucketed token usage data to transform.

preconditions: usage_data is a valid TokenUsageList (may be empty)
postconditions: Output has same length as input
Each data point's total_tokens equals input_tokens + output_tokens
Data points are in the same chronological order as the input
If usage_data is empty, returns empty list
Labels are human-readable time strings derived from period_start

structured_side_effects.description: Pure transformation function with no side effects

functions.name: getActiveProjectsCount
functions.description: Reads OpenClaw project configuration from the filesystem and returns the count of active (non-archived) projects.

error_cases.name: PROJECT_CONFIG_READ_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Project configuration directory could not be read

error_cases.name: PROJECT_CONFIG_PARSE_FAILED
error_cases.condition: Project configuration files are malformed

postconditions: On status='ok', data contains an integer >= 0 representing active project count
On status='empty', no project configuration files found
Archived projects are excluded from the count

structured_side_effects.description: Reads project config files to count active projects

invariants: All monetary values are stored and transmitted as integer microdollars (1 USD = 1,000,000 microdollars); conversion to display dollars happens only in UI presentation layer or in chart transform functions
All timestamps are ISO 8601 strings with timezone information
All data access functions return DataResult — components never access filesystem or SQLite directly
Components never import from OpenClaw or PACT libraries directly; all integration is via filesystem reads and CLI invocations through the data access layer
ActivityEvent IDs are UUID v4 and globally unique; used for SSE deduplication via lastEventId
The SSE endpoint at /api/events is the single multiplexed stream for all event types; no per-type SSE endpoints
Budget alerts are persisted exclusively in SQLite (dashboard-specific data); agent/project/cost data is read from filesystem (OpenClaw/PACT data)
Zod schemas are the single source of truth for all type definitions; TypeScript types are inferred via z.infer<typeof schema>
All external input (API params, file contents, CLI output) is validated through Zod schemas before use
DataResult with status='empty' triggers 'no data available' placeholder UI (AC8); it is not treated as an error
SSE heartbeat events are emitted every 30 seconds to keep connections alive
BudgetAlert evaluation includes debounce logic: an alert is not re-triggered if last_triggered_at is within the same evaluation period
DashboardOverviewResponse sections fail independently: one section's error does not prevent other sections from rendering
Files in this component respect the 250-line limit; types are split across lib/types/activity.ts, cost.ts, budget.ts, dashboard.ts, common.ts, sse.ts with a barrel index.ts
requires: lib/db — SQLite connection via better-sqlite3 for budget alerts and dashboard preferences
lib/sse — SSE event bus for multiplexed event streaming
lib/data — Data access layer (filesystem + SQLite) for all reads
lib/pact — PACT CLI wrapper + file parser for health snapshots
lib/openclaw — OpenClaw config/session reader for agent and project data
